title: lasthenia macrantha subsp. bakeri
intro: Lasthenia macrantha subsp. bakeri, also known as Baker's lasthenia, is a rare and endemic subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of essential oils and terpenes. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, making it a potential treatment for minor skin irritations and infections. Herbal preparations of Baker's lasthenia include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
